codeReview.summary: The feature is implemented as focused regression coverage for the existing sensitive enum runtime seam and nested override paths. The added integration tests explicitly prove the required sensitive-contained enum roundtrip, nested-override coexistence, and mixed save_many isolation behavior, and the stderr refresh matches the current supported secure-container contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker transcript shows repeated attempts to execute .factory/init.sh directly on Windows even though repo/global guidance says .sh files must be read as text and translated to native commands. The rust-library-worker skill mentions reading mission/library files but does not restate the Windows-specific init handling, so the procedure still invites a forbidden/failed step.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl session 318fe76a-299a-4835-88bb-17c8b5544c82 records a direct .factory/init.sh execution attempt during startup; ~/.factory/AGENTS.md says .sh files must not be invoked on Windows; .factory/skills/rust-library-worker/SKILL.md lacks an explicit Windows init translation instruction.

summary: Pass. Commit 376c1a87 adds the missing explicit regressions for VAL-ER-002, VAL-CROSS-003, and VAL-CROSS-005 in core/tests/integration_db.rs, and updates the compile-fail stderr to the current SensitiveValueOf<T> boundary. I found no blocking or non-blocking code issues in the feature scope.
